title: Nehawu workers of NMU embark on strike
bodyText:
Video

GEORGE NEWS & VIDEO - Nehawu-affiliated workers of Nelson Mandela University (NMU) this morning, Monday 26 February, were picketing at the George Campus entrance from early on as part of a wages strike.


> An official handover of the union's demands to management at the university's main campus in Gqeberha was done on Friday 23 February when they threatened with a full-blown strike should their grievances not be attended to.


The union is demanding a wage increase of 8%.

Local shop steward Anathi Masebeni said negotiations have broken down when their wage demand was not acceded to and the  university unilaterally implemented an increase of 6,9%.

He said they are open for further negotiations, but until such time as these resume, the strike will continue.

One of the union's other demands is that the university contribute 60% to workers' medical aid contributions.

Comment from the NMU is being awaited.


> Watch a video below:

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
